# Firmament Example Pack

This directory contains small canonical Firmament examples for the current v1 surface.

These examples represent the currently supported boolean subset.
Other combinations may fail and are intentionally not supported yet.

- `box_basic.firmament` — minimal single-box golden-path example; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/box_basic.step`.
- `cylinder_basic.firmament` — minimal single-cylinder export example; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/cylinder_basic.step`.
- `cone_frustum_basic.firmament` — minimal single frustum-cone export example using the v1 `cone` primitive; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/cone_frustum_basic.step`.
- `cone_pointed_top_zero.firmament` — minimal single pointed-cone export example using the same v1 `cone` primitive with `top_radius: 0`; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/cone_pointed_top_zero.step`.
- `sphere_basic.firmament` — minimal single-sphere export example; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/sphere_basic.step`.
- `torus_basic.firmament` — minimal single-torus export example using the canonical `major_radius` / `minor_radius` surface; helper export paths: `testdata/firmament/exports/torus_basic.step` and the milestone proof artifact `testdata/firmament/exports/m10g2-torus.step`.
- `box_add_basic.firmament` — minimal supported box-plus-box boolean add example; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/box_add_basic.step`.
- `boolean_add_basic.firmament` — canonical proven boolean add example for the current box/box single-box subset; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/boolean_add_basic.step`.
- `ribbed_support_f1.firmament` — bounded F1 connected orthogonal add family example for rib/wall-like parts; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/ribbed_support_f1.step`.
- `boolean_subtract_basic.firmament` — canonical proven boolean subtract example for the current box/box single-box subset; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/boolean_subtract_basic.step`.
- `boolean_intersect_basic.firmament` — canonical proven boolean intersect example for the current box/box single-box subset; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/boolean_intersect_basic.step`.
- `boolean_box_cylinder_hole.firmament` — the only supported box+cylinder boolean case: subtracting a Z-aligned cylinder that cleanly spans the full box height to form a single box-with-through-hole solid; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/boolean_box_cylinder_hole.step`.
- `boolean_box_cone_throughhole_basic.firmament` — the narrow supported box+cone boolean case: subtracting a Z-aligned cone that cleanly spans the full box height, keeps both boundary circles strictly inside the box footprint, and reconstructs as one box-with-conical-through-hole solid; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/boolean_box_cone_throughhole_basic.step`.
- `boolean_two_cylinder_holes_basic.firmament` — canonical sequential safe composition case for `box → subtract cylinder → subtract cylinder`; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/boolean_two_cylinder_holes_basic.step`.
- `boolean_cylinder_cone_holes_basic.firmament` — canonical sequential safe composition case for `box → subtract cylinder → subtract cone`; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/boolean_cylinder_cone_holes_basic.step`.
- `boolean_box_sphere_cavity_basic.firmament` — narrow supported `subtract(box, sphere)` cavity case only: the sphere must remain strictly contained and non-tangent so the result can be reconstructed as one outer shell plus one inner spherical shell; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/boolean_box_sphere_cavity_basic.step`.
- `placed_primitive.firmament` — selector-based placement using `place.on` and `offset[3]`; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/placed_primitive.step`.
- `p1_blind_hole_on_face_semantic.firmament` — minimal semantic placement subset example using `place.on_face` for a blind-hole tool placement.
- `p1_flange_radial_hole_semantic.firmament` — minimal semantic placement subset example using `place.around_axis` + `place.radial_offset` for flange radial hole placement.
- `p1_bracket_rib_on_face_semantic.firmament` — minimal semantic placement subset example using `place.on_face` for bracket rib authoring ergonomics.
- `schema_box_basic.firmament` — minimal schema-present export example showing schema presence without changing export body semantics; helper export path: `testdata/firmament/exports/schema_box_basic.step`.

Unsupported boolean requests are tracked as fixtures/tests, not presented in this working example pack. The supported safe subtract family remains narrow and explicit: `subtract(box, cylinder)` and `subtract(box, cone)` still require a Z-aligned tool, full span through the current box shell, non-degenerate boundary circles for cones, and strict in-bounds clearance with no tangent/grazing contact. Sequential composition is limited to repeated document-order subtracts from that same safe family only: `box → subtract cylinder/cone → subtract cylinder/cone`, while the intermediate result must stay representable as one outer box shell plus non-interfering analytic through-holes. This is not general multi-feature boolean support. Box+cylinder `add`, box+cylinder `intersect`, rotated cylinders, partial-depth cuts, tangent/grazing cases, outside-footprint cuts, interfering multi-hole layouts, and unsupported subtract orderings remain unsupported. Box+cone `add`, box+cone `intersect`, tilted cones, partial-depth cone cuts, tangent/grazing cone cuts, apex-inside cases, outside-footprint cone cuts, and unsupported mixed-ordering requests remain unsupported. Box+sphere support is still narrow and truth-first: only `subtract(box, sphere)` with a strictly contained, non-tangent, fully enclosed cavity is supported, reconstructed via one outer shell plus one inner spherical shell. `add(box, sphere)`, `intersect(box, sphere)`, tangent cavity requests, and partially outside sphere requests remain unsupported. Box+torus booleans are currently unsupported in every audited form: `subtract(box, torus)`, `add(box, torus)`, and `intersect(box, torus)` all fail the real kernel boundary for centered, offset, fully contained, face-intersecting, and outside-footprint torus placements, so no box+torus example is included here. These files are intended to stay minimal, readable, and aligned with the implemented language surface. Export artifacts are kept separately under `testdata/firmament/exports/` so examples remain source-only and demo outputs stay easy to find.
